---
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Where donors live

How far do Hubbard Hall's donors live from the hall? Distances are straight-line (haversine) from
the venue (25 E Main St, Cambridge NY) to each donor household, bucketed into 1 / 10 / 20+ mile
bands. Donors whose only address is a PO box are placed at their ZIP's centroid and flagged
"approximate" (excluded from the tight 1-mile count so it stays honest).

```{python}
#| label: setup-geo
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from hh.io import read_parquet
from hh.viz import style, BLUE

households = read_parquet("processed", "households_summary.parquet")
donors = households[households["is_donor"]]
by_band = (
    donors.groupby("distance_band", observed=True)
    .agg(households=("id", "count"), dollars=("total_donations", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
by_band["share"] = (by_band["dollars"] / by_band["dollars"].sum()).round(3)
by_band
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dollars-band
#| fig-cap: "Donated dollars by distance band"
fig = px.bar(by_band, x="distance_band", y="dollars", color_discrete_sequence=[BLUE])
fig.update_layout(
    showlegend=False, xaxis_title="Distance from Hubbard Hall", yaxis_title="Donated $"
)
style(fig).show()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-households-band
#| fig-cap: "Donor households by distance band"
fig = px.bar(by_band, x="distance_band", y="households", color_discrete_sequence=[BLUE])
fig.update_layout(
    showlegend=False, xaxis_title="Distance from Hubbard Hall", yaxis_title="Donor households"
)
style(fig).show()
```

Giving is overwhelmingly local. The tightest band includes some approximate (ZIP-centroid)
placements — here is the centroid share within each band, to read the 1-mile number in context:

```{python}
#| label: precision-by-band
prec = (
    donors.groupby("distance_band", observed=True)["geo_precision"]
    .apply(lambda s: (s == "centroid").mean())
    .round(2)
    .to_frame("centroid_share")
)
prec
```
